feed.title: The future of the Bitcoin Core GUI

entry.title: The future of the Bitcoin Core GUI
entry.summary:
The discussion revolves around the evolution of the graphical user interface (GUI) for Bitcoin Core, suggesting a significant shift in its development and maintenance strategy. The proposal outlines transitioning the GUI to operate as an independent process that communicates with the main application through inter-process communication (IPC). This approach acknowledges the challenges in quantifying the exact number of users who interact with Bitcoin through the GUI, emphasizing the necessity of retaining this feature for the foreseeable future.
There's a clear preference expressed for keeping the GUI bundled with Bitcoin Core while proposing a change in its management. The suggested model involves maintaining the GUI in a separate repository, akin to how the secp256k1 library is handled. This restructuring aims to segregate the development workload, allowing those uninterested in GUI work to focus on other aspects of Bitcoin Core. Moreover, it highlights the intention to maintain an 'official' Bitcoin Core GUI, ensuring it remains a recognized and integral component of the project despite the proposed administrative changes. This direction not only facilitates focused maintenance but also underscores a commitment to supporting diverse user interactions with Bitcoin Core.
